steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures around the blank
steps.content: The left side contains a noun phrase naming the festival. The right side contains an appositive phrase—introduced by “or” and providing an alternative name with explanation.

steps.title: Apply the dash enclosure rule
steps.content:
The rule is that matching dashes enclose non-restrictive appositives.

An opening dash after “Culture” completes the enclosure signaled by the closing dash before “became”—that's what choice B provides.

The other choices either use a colon (A) or omit the opening dash (C, D), both failing to enclose the appositive properly.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
